reviews.strongestCounterargument: The hierarchical Bernoulli process was designed to expose repository-level effect heterogeneity, so the result may say more about the chosen simulation than about any deployed coding benchmark.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest claim was an implied repository-count cutoff; four balanced cluster-count cells cannot establish a portable threshold for unbalanced real evaluations.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: The 1,200 by 400 nested Monte Carlo run is computationally nontrivial, and directional replication requires preserving the seeded generator, interval definitions, and balanced cluster design.
revisions: Replaced the absolute resample-repositories title and added the explicit falsification that the cluster method did not win across controls.
Removed the 30-to-40 repository heuristic and made method disagreement, rather than cluster count alone, the provisional-use trigger.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: GPT-Live is currently a consumer product release without public benchmark scores or an API contract, so an engineering article risks projecting behavior from earlier realtime systems onto an unavailable deployment target.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest claim was that teams could treat the consumer experience as an API preview; transport, policy, serving snapshot, tools, and enterprise controls may differ materially.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: A faithful duplex evaluation needs timestamped audio and tool events, realistic speakers, transport telemetry, and consented language and accent coverage that many existing voice harnesses do not retain.
revisions: Added an explicit non-transfer boundary between the ChatGPT consumer behavior and any future API model sharing the family name.
Added a sourced requirement-by-requirement decision matrix separating evaluation-now cases from deployment cases that must wait.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: Most headline measurements are provider-reported and use different proof tasks and test-time budgets, so they establish pilot value but not a normalized advantage over other theorem-proving systems.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest claim treated the pass-at-eight lift as a capability gain; correlated retries and early stopping mean it is only a result under a larger search budget.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: Reproducing the results requires exact Lean and Mathlib revisions, Dockerized benchmark tasks, model weights or API access, sampling settings, and substantial proof compilation and review capacity.
revisions: Reframed the 14.3-point pass-at-eight gain as search-budget yield and rejected an independence or causal compute interpretation.
Added the September 30, 2026 Labs endpoint retirement to the adoption boundary and required an export or self-hosting path for pilots.
